pi_items.source: Task description (setup_task.sh line 65), export_result.sh line 52 searches for this channel name by exact match
setup_task.sh line 12 creates directory, line 74 confirms it as destination for valid messages
setup_task.sh line 13 creates directory, line 74 confirms it as destination for invalid messages
Task description and setup_task.sh line 65; verified via HL7 MLLP specification as standard default port per RFC
Pattern verified against test messages: valid (123456, 999999) match pattern, invalid (ABC, BAD123) do not match. Python regex validation confirms all test cases pass.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a Mirth/NextGen Connect channel named 'MRN_Quality_Firewall' listening on TCP port 6661 (the standard HL7 MLLP default port). The channel validates Patient IDs (PID-3.1) against the regex pattern ^\d{6}$ (exactly 6 digits). Valid 6-digit IDs are routed to /home/ga/valid with an 'MRN-' prefix, while invalid IDs are routed to /home/ga/quarantine with an error message. All test data is synthetic, generated by setup_task.sh using two HL7 v2.3 ADT messages: valid_123456.hl7 (ID='123456') and invalid_ABC.hl7 (ID='ABC'). The export_result.sh script runs black-box testing with IDs '999999' (valid) and 'BAD123' (invalid).
